team_context.competitive_window: ascending — won NFC South at 8-9 in 2025, made playoffs for first time since 2017, young core building toward sustained contention
team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$10-12M in effective space after aggressive free agency (Phillips $120M, Lloyd $45M, Moehrig already on books), restructured Derrick Brown to create $13.5M. Several big cap hits (Hunt $24.3M, Horn $24M, Moton $21M). Can create more via additional restructures.
team_context.trade_posture: Buyers building around Bryce Young. Invested heavily in defense this offseason. Would move redundant pieces (Hubbard, Legette) for picks but protective of core. Need OT help with Ekwonu injured.

players.name: Bryce Young
players.performance_evidence: 3,011 passing yards, 23 TDs, 11 INTs, 87.8 passer rating, 63.6% completion rate in 2025. 70.5 PFF overall grade (26th/43 QBs). Led 6 game-winning drives (T-2nd in NFL). Set Panthers franchise record with 448 passing yards in one game. Led team to NFC South title at 8-9. Showed improvement from disastrous 2023-24 but still below-average starter — barely cracked 3,000 yards with double-digit turnovers.

trade_value.reasoning: Former #1 overall pick on an extremely team-friendly deal ($12M cap hit in 2026, 5th-year option picked up for $26.5M in 2027). Only 25 with 2 years of control remaining. Production is below-average starter level but the contract value, age, and upside of a former #1 pick on a cheap deal make him worth a mid-1st. Panthers view him as their franchise QB.

players.availability_reasoning: Panthers just picked up his 5th-year option, building the entire offense around him. Won the NFC South. Dave Canales is committed. Zero chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Missed time in 2025 with ankle injury (Week 8). Small frame (5'10, 204 lbs) raises durability concerns.

players.name: Kenny Pickett
players.performance_evidence: 16-11 career record as starter but has never flourished. 4,953 career passing yards, 16 TDs, 16 INTs in 36 games. Bounced from Steelers to Eagles to Browns to Raiders. 62.2% career completion rate. Minimal production in limited 2025 action with Raiders (188 yards, 1 TD, 2 INT in 6 games).

trade_value.reasoning: Experienced backup QB on a 1-year deal. Former first-rounder with starting experience has some value as insurance but has never established himself. Worth a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as Bryce Young's backup on a 1-year prove-it deal. Panthers would listen if a QB-needy team came calling, but the price would be minimal and they'd need a replacement.

players.name: Chuba Hubbard
players.performance_evidence: Had a breakout 2024 (1,195 yards, 10 TDs, 4.8 YPC) but regressed in 2025 with only 511 rushing yards as Rico Dowdle took over as the primary back. Lost starting job mid-season. Locker room leader but diminished on-field role.

trade_value.reasoning: Affordable contract ($6.3M cap hit) with an easy out, but lost his starting job in 2025 and is now redundant with Jonathon Brooks returning. RB is a devalued position. Worth a 4th-round pick to an RB-needy team. Spotrac noted Panthers could trade him and free up ~$3M in cap.

players.availability_reasoning: Lost starting job to Rico Dowdle in 2025, Jonathon Brooks returning from ACL. Multiple beat reporters have noted trade speculation. Contract has easy out after 2026. Team culture ('one of our guys') makes an in-season trade unlikely but offseason move is plausible for fair value.
players.character_notes: Beloved locker room leader, team captain. Canales and Morgan repeatedly call him 'one of our guys.'
players.trade_buzz: Multiple outlets speculated about Hubbard trade availability during 2025 season after losing starting role. Spotrac flagged him as a trade candidate.

players.name: Jonathon Brooks
players.performance_evidence: Missed entire 2025 season on PUP list recovering from second ACL tear in same knee. Only 3 career NFL games (9 carries, 22 yards). Was first RB drafted in 2024 (46th overall) and showed promise at Texas but has yet to prove anything at the NFL level.

trade_value.reasoning: Unproven 2nd-round pick coming off two ACL tears on the same knee. Cheap rookie deal but massive injury risk. The talent was there pre-injury but teams would be buying potential sight-unseen. Worth a 5th-6th round pick to a team willing to gamble.

players.availability_reasoning: Panthers hedged by signing Dowdle and drafting Etienne while Brooks was out. Two ACL tears on the same knee create legitimate long-term concerns. If he shows well in camp, value rises; if not, Panthers may look to recoup something.
players.injury_notes: Two ACL tears on right knee — first in college (2023), second in Week 14 of 2024 rookie season. Missed entire 2025 season. Major durability question mark.

players.name: Tetairoa McMillan
players.performance_evidence: Won 2025 Offensive Rookie of the Year. Led all rookie WRs with 70 catches, 1,014 yards, and 7 TDs. Only rookie to reach 1,000 receiving yards. Set Panthers rookie receiving record. 6'5, 212 lbs — elite size/athleticism combination. Dane Brugler projects him as a future top-10 WR.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old OROY on a 4-year rookie deal with a 5th-year option. Elite size, production, and trajectory on one of the cheapest deals in the NFL for his output. This is the exact profile that commands multiple 1st-round picks in actual trades. Essentially untradeable at any realistic price.

players.availability_reasoning: Offensive Rookie of the Year, franchise WR1, 4 years of cheap control remaining with a 5th-year option. The Panthers traded up to draft him and he's the centerpiece of their offense alongside Bryce Young. Absolutely untouchable.
players.character_notes: Apologized for racial slur used during esports livestream in early 2026. Minor PR issue, no suspension or league discipline.

players.name: Jalen Coker
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025: 33 catches, 394 yards, 3 TDs in 13 games (missed time with injuries). Had 9 catches for 134 yards and a TD in the playoff loss to the Rams. Outplayed 1st-round pick Legette consistently. 2024 as UDFA: 32 catches, 478 yards, 2 TDs in 11 games. Exclusive rights free agent — essentially locked in at minimum salary.

trade_value.reasoning: Emerging WR2 on an ERFA deal (essentially minimum salary). Has only 2 seasons of NFL tape but trending strongly upward. UDFA pedigree and short track record limit value, but the cheap contract and ascending play make him worth a late 3rd/early 4th. Panthers unlikely to move him.

players.availability_reasoning: ERFA so Panthers control him cheaply. He's become the clear WR2 and a key part of the passing game. Team would need a significant overpay to move their emerging complementary piece to McMillan.
players.injury_notes: Quad injury cost him preseason and early 2025 (IR). Has missed games in both NFL seasons.

players.name: Xavier Legette
players.performance_evidence: Deeply disappointing through 2 NFL seasons. 2025: 35 catches, 363 yards, 3 TDs in 16 games — awful for a 1st-round pick. High drop rate (14% as rookie, continued in Year 2). Made mental errors (stepping out of bounds, lack of awareness). Lost snaps to 6th-round rookie Jimmy Horn Jr. 1 catch for 8 yards in playoff loss. Coaches still express faith publicly but production doesn't match.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick on a cheap rookie deal, but two seasons of poor production and high drop rates have cratered his value. Still only 25 with some physical tools. Worth a 5th-round pick to a team willing to bet on a change of scenery. Dead cap (~$5.5M) complicates a trade slightly.

players.availability_reasoning: Fell to WR3/WR4 behind McMillan and Coker. Multiple outlets have suggested Panthers could trade him after Coker's breakout. The dead cap is manageable. A team change could benefit both parties.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury cost him early games in 2025.
players.trade_buzz: Last Word on Sports and other outlets explicitly suggested Panthers could trade Legette after the playoff loss. ESPN's David Newton noted his diminished role.

players.name: Ja'Tavion Sanders
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 29 catches, 190 yards, 1 TD in 13 games before broken fibula ended season in Week 17. Step back from rookie year (33 catches, 342 yards, 1 TD). Panthers don't emphasize TE in passing game. Still only 23 with athletic upside (4.69 40, 6'4, 252 lbs). Coach Canales pushed him to reshape body entering 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, athletic TE on a cheap 4th-round rookie deal but limited NFL production. The upside is there physically but hasn't translated yet. Worth a late 5th/6th to a TE-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Starting TE on a dirt-cheap deal with upside. Panthers wouldn't move him cheaply but aren't deeply invested in the position either. Would need at least a 4th-rounder to consider.
players.injury_notes: Broken right fibula in Week 17 of 2025, required surgery. Neck injury in 2024. Should be healthy by 2026 training camp.

players.name: Tommy Tremble
players.performance_evidence: Blocking-first TE2. 23 receptions in each of the last two full seasons. Limited receiving production. Stepped up when Sanders was injured. Reliable but not a difference-maker in the passing game.

trade_value.reasoning: Blocking TE on an expiring deal with minimal receiving production. Worth a conditional 7th at best. Was a cut candidate entering the offseason per SI.

players.availability_reasoning: Was listed as a potential cap casualty by SI. Expiring deal, limited production, TE2 behind Sanders. Panthers could move him for late-round pick compensation or release.
players.trade_buzz: SI identified Tremble as a potential cut candidate to free up cap space.

players.name: Ikem Ekwonu
players.performance_evidence: Started at LT since being drafted 6th overall. Allowed 5 sacks in 2025 (7 in 2024). Inconsistent pass protector — PFF ranked him below-average for a 1st-round LT. Better in run blocking. Has started every game when healthy. On 5th-year option at $17.56M fully guaranteed.

trade_value.reasoning: Former top-6 pick with starting experience but hasn't lived up to draft pedigree. On a fully guaranteed $17.56M 5th-year option — acquiring team gets 1 year at a high price with a player coming off a major knee injury. The ruptured patellar tendon is the biggest factor suppressing value. Worth a mid-3rd if healthy but injury risk drops it to a late 3rd/early 4th equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Ruptured patellar tendon in playoff loss makes a trade very unlikely in the near term — who trades for a player who might miss most of 2026? Panthers signed Stone Forsythe as insurance and may draft OT at #19. Extension talks are on hold. The $17.56M is fully guaranteed regardless, so dead cap equals cap hit — no savings from trading.
players.injury_notes: MAJOR: Ruptured patellar tendon in right knee during January 2026 playoff loss. Underwent surgery. Recovery timeline 6-12 months — could miss start of 2026 season or potentially all of it. Previously durable (started every game when healthy).
players.trade_buzz: ESPN's David Newton noted the injury 'could impact Carolina's long-term commitment to Ekwonu.' Extension talks are on hold.

players.name: Damien Lewis
players.performance_evidence: Solid LG who has started since arriving in 2024. Part of an OL unit PFF graded 8th-best in 2024. Reliable in both run blocking and pass protection. Not flashy but consistent. Played LG for all of his Carolina tenure.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting guard on 2 years remaining at $13.25M AAV. That's market rate for a starter. The $7.5M dead cap is manageable. Worth a mid-3rd to a guard-needy team. Panthers could create cap space via extension per reporting.

players.availability_reasoning: Core OL piece, Panthers invested heavily in the line. However, cap hit is significant and an extension could create savings. Not being shopped but has a price if someone overpays.
players.trade_buzz: SI suggested extending Lewis to free up $10M+ in cap space.

players.name: Luke Fortner
players.performance_evidence: Just signed as a free agent in 2026 offseason. Former Jaguars starter. Brings experience and stability to center after Austin Corbett's injury history. Affordable 1-year signing to fill a need.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level center on a cheap 1-year deal. Minimal trade value — worth a conditional 7th at most. Panthers signed him to fill a specific need.

players.availability_reasoning: 1-year signing for depth/competition. If Panthers draft a center or someone else emerges, Fortner becomes expendable. Low investment, low attachment.

players.name: Robert Hunt
players.performance_evidence: Anchor of Carolina's OL. PFF graded Panthers OL 8th-best in 2024 with Hunt as a key piece. Strong in both run blocking and pass protection. Durable — started 55 games in 4 seasons with Dolphins before signing with CAR. Part of an OL group credited with Bryce Young's improvement.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber guard in his prime on a fair contract. However, $24.3M cap hit in 2026 and $25.9M dead cap make a trade essentially impossible — trading team would actually INCREASE their dead cap. Panthers more likely to restructure than trade. If the cap were clean, worth a mid-2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap ($25.9M) exceeds cap hit ($24.3M), making a trade cap-negative. Panthers would lose money trading him. Cornerstone OL piece. Multiple reports suggest restructure is the plan to create cap space, not a trade.
players.trade_buzz: Multiple outlets (ClutchPoints, Cat Scratch Reader) expect a restructure of Hunt's contract, not a trade.

players.name: Taylor Moton
players.performance_evidence: Among top-20 tackles per PFF. Committed just 1 penalty and allowed only 1 sack in 2025. 82 consecutive starts entering 2026. Played every offensive snap for 5+ straight seasons. 8th season — model of consistency and durability. Teammates call him underrated; Robert Hunt said he'd be a Pro Bowler in a bigger market.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite-tier RT in terms of consistency but dead cap ($35M) makes a trade completely impossible. Acquiring team would face a massive cap penalty. If cap were clean, 31-year-old Pro Bowl RT with 2 years left would be worth a late 1st/early 2nd. But the dead cap makes this moot.

players.availability_reasoning: $35M dead cap makes a trade cap-impossible. Panthers signed him to a 2-year extension in 2025 specifically to keep him. Critical to protecting Bryce Young, especially with Ekwonu injured.
players.injury_notes: Knee concerns noted — Panthers declined to extend him early in 2025 offseason partly due to knee worries. Has played through minor issues but no missed time recently.
players.trade_buzz: Was briefly discussed as a free agency candidate before the extension. Now locked in.

players.name: Derrick Brown
players.performance_evidence: First Pro Bowl in 2023 (103 tackles — single-season record for a DL). Franchise cornerstone. 2024 cut short by knee injury. Returned strong in 2025 as the defensive anchor. Sack-fumble helped win crucial Week 13 game vs Rams. Just restructured contract, converting $16.9M into signing bonus with 2 void years. PFF consistently rates him as a top-tier interior DL.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite interior DL in his prime, just restructured his deal. The restructure pushed dead cap to astronomical levels (~$56M), making a trade completely impossible. Even without the dead cap, he's a franchise cornerstone worth multiple 1sts.

players.availability_reasoning: Just restructured his contract to help the team sign Phillips and Lloyd — dead cap is now ~$56M. Face of the defense alongside Jaycee Horn. Panthers built the entire defensive rebuild around him. Zero chance.
players.injury_notes: Suffered knee injury in 2024 that ended his season. Returned fully healthy for 2025.

players.name: Bobby Brown III
players.performance_evidence: Signed 3-year, $21M deal with Panthers in 2025 free agency from Rams. Solid interior run defender. Part of the defensive overhaul. Played a rotational/starting role in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Average starting DT on a fair deal. Dead cap exceeds trade savings making a move unlikely in Year 1. Worth a 4th-5th rounder in theory but practically untradeable due to cap structure of new contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in 2025 free agency. Part of the defensive rebuild. Panthers wouldn't move him unless overpaid — still settling into the scheme.

players.name: Tershawn Wharton
players.performance_evidence: Career-best 6.5 sacks in 2024 with Chiefs, plus 2 postseason sacks. Led KC in combined sacks (8.5). 2-time Super Bowl champion. 29 tackles, 11 QB hits in 2024. Signed 3-year, $45.05M deal ($30.25M guaranteed) with Panthers. Versatile interior pass rusher who excels as 3-technique. UDFA success story from Missouri S&T (Division II).

trade_value.reasoning: Productive interior pass rusher on a new deal. Dead cap ($24M) far exceeds cap hit ($12M) in 2026, making a trade impossible in Year 1. If tradeable, would be worth a high 3rd based on production and contract. Practically untouchable due to cap structure.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a massive deal in 2025 FA. Dead cap ($24M) vs cap hit ($12M) makes a trade cap-destructive. Cornerstone of the Panthers' interior pass rush rebuild.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in 2022 with Chiefs, missed most of that season. Has been fully healthy since.

players.name: Nic Scourton
players.performance_evidence: Second-round pick in 2025. Played a rotational EDGE role as a rookie in Carolina's 3-4 defense. College: 17 sacks across Purdue and Texas A&M. First-team All-SEC in 2024. Still developing at the NFL level — part of the Panthers' long-term pass-rush plan alongside Phillips.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap EDGE rusher on a 4-year rookie deal. Even with modest rookie production, the draft pedigree (2nd round), age (22), and 4 years of team control on a $2.15M AAV contract give him real value. Worth a 3rd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap rookie deal with 4 years of control. Panthers invested a 2nd-round pick and view him as part of the long-term EDGE rotation with Phillips. Would need an overpay (2nd-round pick or better) to move him.

players.name: Devin Lloyd
players.performance_evidence: 2025 Second-team All-Pro and first Pro Bowl. Career-high 5 INTs (including 99-yard pick-six). 81 tackles, 10 QB hits, 7 pass breakups. PFF 88.8 overall grade — 3rd-best LB in the NFL (behind only Fred Warner and Jack Campbell). Thrived in new role with more pass-rushing and less coverage. Dramatic improvement from underwhelming first 3 seasons in Jacksonville.

trade_value.reasoning: All-Pro caliber LB on a 3-year deal at $15M AAV (below market — PFF projected $16M AAV). 27 years old in his prime. Dead cap ($20M) vs cap hit ($12M) makes a 2026 trade cap-negative. If tradeable, worth a late 1st based on elite 2025 production and age. Practically untradeable in Year 1.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed this week as a marquee free agent addition. Dead cap ($20M) far exceeds cap hit ($12M). Panthers specifically targeted him to fix their worst position group. Zero chance of trade.

players.name: Trevin Wallace
players.performance_evidence: Took over as defensive play-caller (green dot) mid-2025 and defense improved. 36 solo tackles, 2 sacks in 2025. 4.51 40-yard speed for a LB. Started as rookie in 2024 when Shaq Thompson went down (64 tackles, 1 sack, 2 FF in 13 games). Had shoulder surgery after 2024. Still developing but coaches praise his growth and communication.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap, athletic LB on a 3-year rookie deal who is the defensive play-caller. With Lloyd's arrival, Wallace may shift to a complementary role but his speed and cheap contract have value. Worth a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: With Lloyd signed, Wallace's role may shift but he's still a core defensive piece as the play-caller. Panthers value his athleticism and growth. Cheap contract makes him easy to keep. Would need a 3rd+ to consider moving him.
players.injury_notes: Shoulder surgery after 2024 season. Returned healthy for 2025.
players.trade_buzz: SI's Schuyler Callihan noted 'I'm not sure Trevin Wallace should be starting' even with Lloyd, suggesting he may be more of a rotational piece going forward.

players.name: Jaelan Phillips
players.performance_evidence: 5 total sacks in 2025 (3 with Dolphins, 2 with Eagles in 8 games). Ranked 9th in NFL with 73 pressures per PFF. 28 total career sacks in 5 seasons, peaking with 8.5 as a rookie in 2021. Explosive athlete with coverage ability. Contract ($30M AAV) prices him as an elite EDGE but production hasn't consistently matched — being paid on potential after proving he can stay healthy for a full season in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old former 1st-round EDGE on a brand-new 4-year, $120M deal. The $80M in guarantees and $65M dead cap make a trade completely impossible for the foreseeable future. If the cap were clean, his age and talent would command a 1st-round pick, but injury history and inconsistent production temper that.

players.availability_reasoning: Literally just signed this week to a 4-year, $120M deal with $80M guaranteed. Dead cap would be ~$65M. This is the Panthers' marquee defensive signing. Completely untradeable.
players.injury_notes: MAJOR HISTORY: Torn Achilles (2023), torn ACL (2024). Missed most of 2 seasons. Played all 17 games in 2025 for first time since 2022. The $120M bet is largely on his health staying intact.

players.name: Mike Jackson
players.performance_evidence: Elite 2025 breakout: 5 INTs (T-2nd in NFL), 19 passes deflected (T-1st in NFL), 85.8 PFF coverage grade (1st among all CBs). 144 total tackles and 36 PDs over 34 games since joining Panthers. Named to PFF 101 at #48. Played 99% of defensive snaps. Massive steal from the Seahawks trade (cost a 7th-round pick equivalent). Now the 2nd-best CB on the team.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber CB on a wildly team-friendly deal ($5.25M AAV). However, only 1 year remaining and turning 30 this offseason. Extension candidate — Spotrac projects $13.4M/year market value. If traded, worth a late 2nd/early 3rd based on elite 2025 production, but expiring deal and age cap the ceiling.

players.availability_reasoning: Massively outperforming his contract. Panthers extended him this offseason on a 2-year deal but he could command more on the open market. Very unlikely to be traded given he and Horn are 'one of the best duos in the league' per SI, but if a team offered a 2nd-rounder, Carolina would have to think hard.
players.injury_notes: Ribs issue during 2025 season, played through it.
players.trade_buzz: Joe Person of The Athletic discussed a potential extension. Panthers signed him to a new 2-year, $10.5M deal this offseason, suggesting commitment.

players.name: Jaycee Horn
players.performance_evidence: 2-time Pro Bowler (2024, 2025). Highest-paid DB in NFL history at time of signing ($25M AAV). 2024: Career-high 68 tackles, 13 PDs, 2 sacks, 1 INT in 15 games. Played 98% of snaps. 2025: Continued elite play alongside Mike Jackson, forming the league's best CB duo by PDs. Allowed only 51% completion rate as nearest defender (2nd in NFL). 26 years old, ascending.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite young CB on a long-term deal. 26 years old, 2x Pro Bowler, franchise cornerstone. Dead cap (~$55M) makes a trade mathematically impossible. Even hypothetically, would command multiple 1sts. Top-5 CB in the NFL.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 4-year, $100M extension making him the highest-paid DB in NFL history. Franchise defensive cornerstone alongside Derrick Brown. Dead cap is astronomical. GM Dan Morgan called him 'the focal point of the defense.'
players.injury_notes: Missed 29 games due to injuries in first 3 seasons (foot, ribs, various). Played 15 games in 2024 and proved durability. Hip injury late in 2024. 2025 season was his healthiest.

players.name: Chau Smith-Wade
players.performance_evidence: Stepped into the nickel CB role in 2025 as a second-year player. 43 solo tackles, 1 INT. Played ~46% of defensive snaps in a rotational role that expanded as the season went on. Teammates and coaches praise his development. Still growing into the role but has earned the starting nickel spot.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap nickel CB on a rookie deal with 3 years of control. Starter-level production in Year 2. Limited pedigree (4th-round pick) and modest stats keep value moderate. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Developing young player on a cheap deal in a key role. Panthers invested in their CB room and Smith-Wade is the nickel piece. Wouldn't move him unless significant return offered.

players.name: Tre'von Moehrig
players.performance_evidence: Coming off career year with Raiders in 2024: 104 tackles, 10 PDs, 2 INTs, 5 TFLs. PFF graded him 6th-best safety against the run (87.5). Extremely durable — 66 of 68 possible games in 4 seasons. Versatile — played box, free safety, slot, and boundary. In first year with Panthers (2025), started every game. Terrific run defender but coverage remains a question (65.7% completion rate allowed, 99.1 passer rating).

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting safety on a 2-year deal at $17M AAV (5th-highest paid S). Dead cap ($28M) far exceeds cap hit in Year 1, making a trade impossible. If tradeable, worth a mid-3rd based on solid production, age, and 2 years of control. Slightly overpaid per some analysts.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap ($28M) makes a trade impossible in 2026. Just signed a major deal in 2025 free agency. Panthers desperately needed a starting safety and invested heavily.
players.injury_notes: Served a 1-game suspension early in 2025 season. Otherwise extremely durable.
players.trade_buzz: Bleacher Report's Kristopher Knox listed his deal as one of the worst in the NFL, but no trade speculation.

players.name: Lathan Ransom
players.performance_evidence: 2025 rookie from Ohio State. Played a rotational role at free safety, seeing ~29% of defensive snaps in games played. Made game-sealing INT vs Tampa Bay Buccaneers in Week 16 win. College playoff experience at Ohio State. Coaches praise his passion and film study. Still developing but has shown flashes.

trade_value.reasoning: Developmental rookie safety on a cheap 4-year deal. Limited NFL tape but showed promise. 4th-round pedigree and rotational role keep value modest. Worth a 6th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap developmental piece with 4 years of control. Panthers drafted him for the future and he's part of the safety rotation. No reason to move him unless a team significantly overpays.

players.name: Nick Scott
players.performance_evidence: Journeyman safety who started with the Rams (won Super Bowl LVI), then spent time with the Bengals and Panthers. Adequate range at free safety but not a playmaker — limited interception production, inconsistent tackling angles. Has started when called upon but grades out below average among full-time starting safeties per PFF (grades typically in the 55-62 range). Adequate depth/bridge starter but not someone teams are building around.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starter on an expiring minimum-level deal with no guaranteed money remaining. A 7th-round pick at best; most teams would simply sign a comparable safety off the street rather than trade for him.

players.availability_reasoning: Carolina is in a rebuild and Scott is a stopgap veteran on a one-year deal. If the Panthers draft or develop a younger safety option, Scott becomes entirely expendable. No dead cap means zero cost to move him. He's not being actively marketed but would be released or traded for anything of value if the roster spot was needed.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in 2022 with the Rams which cost him significant time. Has been healthy since returning but the injury history is a mild flag for a safety relying on range.
